title: trifolium ochroleucon subsp. ochroleucon
intro: Trifolium ochroleucon subsp. ochroleucon, commonly known as pale yellow clover, is a perennial legume plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal medicine, due to its rich content of isoflavones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of pale yellow clover include its potential to help manage menopausal symptoms, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system. Pale yellow clover can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine as a poultice or salve for topical applications.
